text: Mobile app development requires careful planning and technical skill. A key principle is responsive design, which ensures an app works well on different screen sizes, from phones to tablets. Developers must also choose between native and cross-platform approaches. Native apps are built for a specific system like iOS or Android, offering better performance and full access to device features. Cross-platform tools, such as React Native, allow one codebase to run on multiple systems, but performance may sometimes be lower. Once the app is ready, it is submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store. These platforms review apps for quality and safety before they become available to users.
